title: lilium pardalinum subsp. pitkinense
intro: Lilium pardalinum subsp. pitkinense, also known as Pitkin's fawn lily, is a subspecies of the western lily (Lilium pardalinum) that is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pitkin's fawn lily include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautious and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
